formulas.name: Preparation of Primary, Secondary and Tertiary Amines
formulas.notes: Primary amines can be prepared from alkyl halides through reaction with ammonia, while secondary and tertiary amines are prepared through the reaction of alkyl halides with secondary and tertiary amines respectively.

formulas.name: Reaction of Amines with Nitrous Acid
formulas.notes: Primary amines form diazonium salts, while secondary amines form hydroxylamine and tertiary amines do not react with nitrous acid.

formulas.name: Reaction of Amines with Sodium or Potassium Nitrite
formulas.notes: Primary amines form diazonium salts, while secondary and tertiary amines form azo compounds.

formulas.name: Electrophilic Substitution in Aniline
formulas.notes: Aniline undergoes electrophilic substitution at the meta position due to the presence of the amino group, which is an activator and an orienting group.

formulas.name: Amines as Bases
formulas.variables: pKa = acid dissociation constant, pKb = base dissociation constant
formulas.notes: Stronger bases have lower pKa values.

formulas.name: Basic Strength of Amines
formulas.variables: pKa = acid dissociation constant, pKb = base dissociation constant
formulas.notes: Primary amines are stronger bases than secondary and tertiary amines.

formulas.name: Acidic Strength of Amines
formulas.variables: pKa = acid dissociation constant, Ka = acid dissociation constant
formulas.notes: pKa is a measure of the strength of an acid.

formulas.name: Solubility of Amines
formulas.notes: Amines are generally soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding, but their solubility can be affected by the presence of other functional groups.
